authHero.title: WhatsApp communication at scale, without blocks.
authHero.subtitle: Smart distribution across lines, per-channel limits and real-time monitoring.

docs.title: Integration documentation
docs.subtitle: Connect your systems to send messages through the platform.
docs.jsonNote: All requests and responses use JSON (UTF-8). Sending is asynchronous: the API accepts the message and delivers it in the background.
docs.authDesc: Authenticate every request with your API Key, using the Authorization header with the Bearer scheme, or the x-api-key header. Generate and revoke your keys on this screen; the full secret is shown only once when created.
docs.authNote: Treat your API Key like a password. Never include it in client-side code (browser/app); use it only from your server.
docs.sendDesc: Create a message. The API responds 202 Accepted and queues it for delivery.
docs.contentTypesDesc: The content.type field supports: text, image, video, audio, document, sticker. For media, content.text acts as a caption (optional). content.media accepts a public URL or the file in base64.
docs.responseDesc: An accepted request returns 202 with the message identifier:
docs.lifecycleDesc: The status evolves asynchronously. You can follow it in real time under Reports.
docs.errorsDesc: Errors return a stable identifier in the code field, along with a human-readable message.
docs.f_to: Recipient number in international format, digits only (no + sign). E.g.: 573001112233.
docs.f_content: Object with the message content (see details below).
docs.f_contentType: Content type: text, image, video, audio, document or sticker.
docs.f_text: Message text. Required for type=text; used as a caption for media.
docs.f_media: Public URL or base64 of the file. Required when type is not text.
docs.f_mimeType: File MIME type (optional; inferred when possible).
docs.f_replyTo: Number the recipient should reply to (optional). If present, a 'reply to' line with a https://wa.me/ link is appended at the end of the message, in your account language.
docs.f_quoted: Identifier of a previous message to quote (optional).
docs.f_channelId: Force sending through a specific line. If omitted, the platform picks one automatically.
docs.f_externalId: Your unique identifier for the message, for idempotency and reconciliation.
docs.f_metadata: Free-form object stored and returned in reports.
docs.e_duplicate: A message with that externalId already exists.
docs.bp1: Sending is asynchronous: don't wait for the final status in the response. Check it with GET /v1/messages/{id} or in Reports.
docs.bp2: Use externalId to avoid duplicate sends (idempotency) if you retry a request.
docs.bp3: Each line has per-minute and per-day limits; messages are distributed automatically across your connected lines.
docs.bp4: On a 429 or a 5xx error, retry with incremental backoff (exponential).

playground.title: Send tester
playground.subtitle: Send a real test message using your API Key and try different scenarios.
playground.warning: This sends a real message using the selected API Key.

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Your account preferences
settings.accountDesc: Edit your name and email address.
settings.passwordDesc: Change your password. You'll need your current password.
settings.observationDesc: Text appended in italics at the end of every sent message, explaining why the recipient is getting messages through this line.
settings.observationHint: Shown at the end of every send, under the title "Note". Leave empty to append nothing.
settings.observationPlaceholder: E.g.: This line is for order notifications only.
